100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 7, 1926, the British Parliament debated the future of the coal industry amidst ongoing strikes, highlighting tensions between labor and government. Meanwhile, in the United States, the Ford Motor Company announced plans to expand its production facilities, signaling optimism in the post-war economy. In international news, Italy's Mussolini continued to consolidate power, cracking down on dissent as he solidified his fascist regime.
Updated: Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 06:18:10 AM CST
